files.00_Overview.md: Add Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways sections. This overview has good breadth but redundantly covers all three algorithms in detail when it should focus on the 'Why' and high-level 'What' - move detailed algorithm explanations to dedicated files and keep only summary tables here.
files.01_Mathematical_Foundation.md: Add Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways. Good 3W coverage but section 5 (Under-Relaxation) duplicates content from 02_SIMPLE_Algorithm.md - keep this section theoretical and move practical settings to the algorithm files. The solver settings section belongs in 05_Algorithm_Comparison.md instead.
files.02_SIMPLE_Algorithm.md: Add Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways. Section 1 (Mathematical Foundation) heavily overlaps with 01_Mathematical_Foundation.md - consolidate by referencing the math file instead of re-deriving equations. Under-relaxation image reference shows 'IMG_03_004.JPg' with inconsistent capitalization - fix filename.
files.03_PISO_and_PIMPLE_Algorithms.md: Add Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways. Missing the introductory 'Why this matters' context block present in other files. Section 6 (Troubleshooting) duplicates content from 05_Algorithm_Comparison.md - consolidate unique guidance here and reference the comparison file for comprehensive tables.
files.04_Rhie_Chow_Interpolation.md: Add Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways. The checkerboard ASCII art visualization is valuable - retain. Section 6 (Comparison: Staggered vs Collocated) is good but could reference back to 01_Mathematical_Foundation.md for additional theoretical context. No major redundancies detected.
files.05_Algorithm_Comparison.md: This file is structurally sound as a reference document but adds redundant detailed settings sections that duplicate content from 02-04 files. Keep only the unique comparative tables, decision flowchart, and cross-reference to individual algorithm files for implementation details. Add Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways.

global_strategy: Apply consistent 3W Framework (What/Why/How) across all files: each file needs explicit Learning Objectives at the start and Key Takeaways at the end. Eliminate redundancy by having Overview focus on 'Why', Mathematical Foundation focus on theoretical 'What/How', algorithm-specific files (02-04) focus on implementation 'How', and Comparison (05) serve as decision-making reference. Centralize shared content (equation derivations, solver settings) and use cross-references. Standardize section heading hierarchy and ensure all 'Concept Check' questions align with Learning Objectives.
